description_long: This is a long-handled shovel with a D-grip handle at the top, constructed primarily from wood and metal. The handle is straight and appears to be made of varnished wood, showing a natural brown color with a smooth finish. The D-grip is metallic, matching the blade in color and material, and is attached securely to the top of the handle. The blade is square-shaped, flat, and made of dark metal, with a slightly worn and matte appearance, suggesting it is designed for scooping or shoveling flat materials. The blade is attached to the handle with a reinforced socket, and the overall construction looks robust and utilitarian. There are no visible brand markings, mounting holes, or decorative features, and the shovel shows some minor discoloration and surface wear on the blade, indicating moderate use.
description: A long-handled shovel with a D-grip metal handle and a square, flat metal blade, showing minor wear and a utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the shovel, the D-grip handle, and the square metal blade facing forward.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thin profile of the blade and handle, with the D-grip and blade edge visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the D-grip handle and the flat rear surface of the blade.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the slim profile of the handle and blade, similar to view 1 but from the other side.
attribute_handle_type: D-grip handle, metallic, attached to a straight wooden shaft
attribute_blade_type: Square, flat metal blade with a straight edge and reinforced socket
attribute_wear_pattern: Minor discoloration and surface wear on the blade, especially near the edge; handle appears slightly worn but intact
